description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who needs to prepare a small text-classification
dataset for training.  
A raw, tab-separated file is already present at

    /home/user/datasets/raw_reviews.tsv

Your job:

1. Create a new directory exactly named  
   /home/user/processed  

2. Using *only* one or more calls to **awk** and/or **sed** (no Python, Perl, etc.):

   a. Read /home/user/datasets/raw_reviews.tsv.  
   b. Ignore every line that is either  
      • a comment line (begins with a literal “#” in column 1)  
      • an empty / whitespace-only line.  
   c. From the remaining lines keep **only the first three columns**  
      (column separator is TAB).  
   d. Convert the entire contents of column 2 (“text”) to lower-case, leaving
      columns 1 and 3 unchanged.  
   e. Preserve the original intra-field spacing and keep TAB as the column
      separator.  
   f. Write the result, in order, to a new file  

        /home/user/processed/reviews_cleaned.tsv

3. Produce a plain-text log file

        /home/user/processed/processing.log

   containing exactly three lines (no extra spaces) in the exact order shown
   below, using the exact lowercase keys and equals signs:

        input_lines=<TOTAL number of lines that were in the raw file>
        removed_lines=<NUMBER of lines skipped because they were comments or blank>
        output_lines=<NUMBER of lines actually written to reviews_cleaned.tsv>

   Example (format only, numbers will differ):

        input_lines=42
        removed_lines=8
        output_lines=34

Important output-format notes the automated tests will check:

• reviews_cleaned.tsv must exist, be UTF-8 plain text, and contain only the
  cleaned data lines described in step 2, one line per review, terminated with
  a single trailing newline.  
• processing.log must contain exactly the three key=value lines, in that exact
  order, with no leading or trailing spaces, and must end with a single
  newline.  
• The key names must be all lowercase exactly as specified.

Do everything from the Linux shell: create the directory, run your sed/awk
pipeline, and verify the resulting files.  
When you are finished, both files must be in /home/user/processed with the
precise content and naming described above.
truth:
Initial file present before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/datasets/raw_reviews.tsv  
Content (each “\t” is a literal TAB; every line ends with a single “\n”):

#id\ttext\tlabel\textra\n
1\tHello World\tPOSITIVE\tmeta1\n
2\tThis is great\tPOSITIVE\tmeta2\n
#3\tbad data\tNEGATIVE\n
3\tAwful\tNEGATIVE\tmeta3\n
4\tLovely Day\tPOSITIVE\tmeta4\n
\n
5\tterrible\tNEGATIVE\tmeta5\n

Ground-truth that must exist after the agent completes the task:

1. Directory /home/user/processed exists (700 or 755 permissions are fine).

2. /home/user/processed/reviews_cleaned.tsv  
   Exact content:

1\thello world\tPOSITIVE\n
2\tthis is great\tPOSITIVE\n
3\tawful\tNEGATIVE\n
4\tlovely day\tPOSITIVE\n
5\tterrible\tNEGATIVE\n

3. /home/user/processed/processing.log  
   Exact content (final newline required):

input_lines=8\n
removed_lines=3\n
output_lines=5\n
name: task_000000_b55b5cbc
